prerequisites: Network access to GitHub, which hosts the public-apis directory at https://github.com/public-apis/public-apis.
No account, credentials, or API key are required: the source reads a publicly available, curated directory of free APIs.
This connector exposes no configuration fields, so no host, database name, or scope values need to be supplied.

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: The connector defines no secret fields. It reads from the publicly hosted public-apis directory on GitHub, which requires no API key, token, or credentials to access.

setupSteps.title: Confirm connectivity to the public source
setupSteps.body: Ensure the machine running pm can reach GitHub, where the public-apis directory lives (https://github.com/public-apis/public-apis). Because the data is public, no sign-in or credential setup is needed.

setupSteps.title: Run the connector with no configuration
setupSteps.body: This source has no secret fields and no config fields to fill in. Add it to your pm pipeline and run it as-is; pm reads the curated list of public APIs directly from the published directory.

sources.title: public-apis/public-apis: A collective list of free APIs
